note: Required gpt-5.6-sol medium consultation after v4: exact C1 direct-DR, C2 paired converse, C3 analytic-outgoing finite-incoming ball, and closest-comparator repair.
directive:
## DIRECTIVE

Retain the field-level novelty floor and the requested transition-\(L^1\)-Hölder kernel. Replace the current promissory C1–C3 objects as follows.

**C1 — concrete direct-backward sequential DR/HOIF estimator.** Remove \(\widehat g,\widehat T\), the current \(\mathcal R_{12}\), and any instruction to regress \(\widehat m_2(H_2)\) alone. Use nested cross-fitting. On a nuisance fold fit \(\widehat\pi_1,\widehat\pi_2,\widehat m_2\); on an independent backward-regression fold form
\[
\widehat D_2
=\widehat m_2(H_2)+{A_2\over\widehat\pi_2(H_2)}
 \{Y-\widehat m_2(H_2)\},
\]
and regress \(\widehat D_2\) on \(H_1\) among \(A_1=1\), using a boundary-corrected wavelet sieve, to obtain \(\widehat m_1\). On the evaluation fold define
\[
\widehat\psi^{\rm SDR}
=\mathbb P_n\!\left[
 \widehat m_1(H_1)+{A_1\over\widehat\pi_1(H_1)}
 \{\widehat D_2-\widehat m_1(H_1)\}\right].
\]
Define, conditional on the nuisance fold,
\[
b_1^\dagger(h_1)
=E_P[\widehat D_2\mid H_1=h_1,A_1=1].
\]
State the exact bias identity
\[
E_P[\widehat\psi^{\rm SDR}-\psi\mid{\cal T}]
=
E_{Q_1}\!\left[
 \left(1-{\pi_1\over\widehat\pi_1}\right)
 (\widehat m_1-b_1^\dagger)\right]
+
E_{Q_2}\!\left[
 \left(1-{\pi_2\over\widehat\pi_2}\right)
 (\widehat m_2-m_2)\right],
\]
where \(Q_1=P_{H_1}\) and
\[
Q_2(dh_2)=\int P_{H_1}(dh_1)g(h_2\mid h_1)\,dh_2.
\]
This is the required elimination of the pseudo-outcome mixed remainder: there is no term \((\pi_1-\widehat\pi_1)\widehat T(\widehat m_2-m_2)\).

Define the HOIF addition as the sum of two standard projected bilinear-remainder corrections, plus the sequential transport correction needed to implement \(Q_2\) from observed trajectories:

1. project the first displayed bilinear product in \(L^2(Q_1)\);
2. project the second in \(L^2(Q_2)\), representing \(Q_2\)-integration by cross-fitted \(A_1/\widehat\pi_1(H_1)\) weights;
3. correct the resulting transport-weight residual by the chained period-1 score kernel.

Use boundary-corrected wavelet vectors \(Z_{j,K_j}\), independently estimated empirical Gram matrices
\[
\widehat\Omega_1=\mathbb P_{\cal G}[Z_{1,K_1}Z_{1,K_1}^{T}],\qquad
\widehat\Omega_2=\mathbb P_{\cal G}\!\left[
 {A_1\over\widehat\pi_1(H_1)}Z_{2,K_2}Z_{2,K_2}^{T}\right],
\]
and kernels \(K_j(x,x')=Z_{j,K_j}(x)^T\widehat\Omega_j^{-1}Z_{j,K_j}(x')\). Write every order-\(q\) correction as an explicit symmetrized degenerate U-statistic chain of observable propensity and outcome/pseudo-outcome residuals joined by these kernels. State its sign, normalization \((n)_q^{-1}\), and fold provenance. Do not leave “derive a correction” as a definition.

Set
\[
K_j\asymp n^{\,2d/\{2s_j+d\}},\qquad
s_1=\alpha_1+t,\quad s_2=\alpha_2+\beta,
\]
with block-truncation if required for \(K_j>n\). Define \(q_j\) as the least integer satisfying the displayed preliminary-nuisance remainder inequality needed to make the uncorrected Neumann tail \(o(n^{-r_j})\); the proposal must state that inequality and the nuisance rates used in it. The higher-order target is the two projected stage-local bilinear remainders and the observable \(Q_2\)-transport residual—not estimation of \(g\), \(T\), or \(\widehat T(\widehat m_2-m_2)\).

**C2 — two explicit calibrated fuzzy experiments.** Replace the present single mixture by two separately calibrated finite experiments, one for each exponent. For both, state the entire longitudinal factorization
\[
p_1(h_1)p(a_1\mid h_1)p(h_2\mid h_1,a_1)
p(a_2\mid h_2,a_1)p(y\mid h_2,a_1,a_2),
\]
all untreated/off-regime factors, the binary-\(Y\) probabilities, and an explicit full-data extension constructed from independent exogenous uniforms so consistency and both exchangeability statements hold.

For the \(\alpha_1+t\) experiment fix \(p_1\equiv1\), \(\pi_2\equiv1/2\), a bounded analytic mean-zero \(v(h_2)\), and a fixed bounded \(m_2=\delta v\). With disjoint incoming bumps \(u_{j,h}\), define
\[
\pi_{1,z}(h_1)=\frac12+\eta\sum_jz_ju_{j,h}(h_1),
\]
\[
g_{z,\lambda}(h_2\mid h_1)
=1+\epsilon\sum_j
 {\lambda_j u_{j,h}(h_1)\over1+2\eta z_ju_{j,h}(h_1)}\,v(h_2).
\]
Disjoint support makes this unambiguous, \(\int g\,dh_2=1\), and
\[
\pi_{1,z}g_{z,\lambda}
=\pi_{1,z}+{\epsilon\over2}\sum_j\lambda_ju_{j,h}v
\]
on each bump. Mix uniformly over \(z\), with \(\lambda_j=+z_j\) under \(\nu_+\) and \(\lambda_j=-z_j\) under \(\nu_-\). State the exact target separation
\[
\left|E_{\nu_+}\psi-E_{\nu_-}\psi\right|
=
4\eta\epsilon\delta\!\left(\int v^2\right)
\sum_j\int {u_{j,h}^2\over1-4\eta^2u_{j,h}^2}
\asymp\eta\epsilon.
\]
Calibrate
\[
J\asymp h^{-d},\quad
\eta=c_\eta h^{\alpha_1},\quad
\epsilon=c_\epsilon h^t,\quad
h\asymp n^{-2/\{2(\alpha_1+t)+d\}},
\]
and state the affinity calculation to be proved as
\[
n^2\eta^2\epsilon^2/J=O(1).
\]
This yields separation \(n^{-2(\alpha_1+t)/\{2(\alpha_1+t)+d\}}\).

For the \(\alpha_2+\beta\) experiment fix the first-period law and transition. At \(H_2\), use the analogous exactly paired construction
\[
\pi_{2,z}=\frac12+a\sum_jz_jv_{j,h},\qquad
m_{2,z,\lambda}
=b\sum_j{\lambda_jv_{j,h}\over1+2az_jv_{j,h}},
\]
with \(Y\in\{-1,1\}\) having conditional mean \(m_{2,z,\lambda}\), and mix over \(\lambda=\pm z\). Set
\[
J\asymp h^{-d},\quad a\asymp h^{\alpha_2},\quad
b\asymp h^\beta,\quad
h\asymp n^{-2/\{2(\alpha_2+\beta)+d\}},
\]
state \(n^2a^2b^2/J=O(1)\), and compute the exact separation \(\asymp ab=n^{-r_2}\).

For each experiment list positivity bounds, Hölder norm bounds, transition normalization, \(Y\)-probability validity, component-law membership, the mixing measures, product mixtures \(\int P_\theta^{\otimes n}d\nu_\pm\), and the exact Hellinger/chi-square quantity to be bounded. Delete the baseline \(H_1\)-density perturbation. It is invalid for the claimed first exponent unless its amplitude is explicitly included in the target and affinity calibration; doing so produces a three-smoothness signal and does not establish the stated \(\alpha_1+t\) rate.

**C3 — explicit finite-\(t\) structural-separation node.** Add a separately numbered proposition/conjecture defining
\[
{\cal G}^{t_0}_{\rm AH}(M,\theta_0)
=
\left\{1+\theta w(h_1)v_0(h_2):
 \|w\|_{C^{t_0}}\le M,\ |\theta|\le\theta_0\right\},
\]
where \(t_0>d/2\) is fixed and finite, \(v_0(h_2)=\sqrt2\cos(2\pi h_{2,1})\) is analytic, bounded, and mean-zero, and \(\theta_0\) ensures positive densities. Make the class genuinely nontrivial by allowing an infinite-dimensional \(C^{t_0}\) ball for \(w\) and exhibit an exact-\(t_0\) member, for noninteger \(t_0\), using a smooth cutoff times \(|h_{1,1}-1/2|^{t_0}\), which is not in \(C^{t_0+\xi}\) for any specified \(\xi>0\). Do not call a wholly analytic incoming-state class “finite smoothness”: analytic functions are smoother than every finite Hölder order. State explicitly that on this non-state-independent subclass, if \(\alpha_2+\beta\ge d/2\), then the proposed frontier predicts root-\(n\) estimation for every \(\alpha_1>0\), because \(\alpha_1+t_0>d/2\).

**Closest-comparator and consumer repair.** Add Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele (2025, EcoSta Abstract A0576) as the closest comparator and use an object-by-object table: model regularity, whether a transition density is estimated, recursive pseudo-outcome correction, stated rate channels, lower bound, and structural separation. Their public abstract establishes a two-time-point g-formula HOIF analysis under anisotropic and additive structure with minimax optimality in some settings; it does not expose enough assumptions for a lawful set-inclusion claim. Therefore claim only the exact additions stated here: incoming-state \(C^t(L^1(dh_2))\) transition regularity without outgoing-state density smoothness, direct-DR backward regression without fitting \(g\), the two-channel \((\alpha_1+t,\alpha_2+\beta)\) frontier, and the legally normalized chained converse. Do not claim strict non-inclusion in their class unless a full manuscript supplies the assumptions needed to prove it.

Replace every generic “consumer” for C1, C2, and C3 with: “the model-specific non-root-\(n\) optimality problem for time-varying treatment effects posed by Cinelli, Feller, Imbens, Kennedy, Magliacane, and Zubizarreta (2025, §3.5.3), instantiated for the named two-decision physiologic-challenge regime.” Do not lower the novelty floor.

## RATIONALE

The direct DR pseudo-outcome changes the first regression’s population target from \(Tm_2\) to \(b_1^\dagger\). That produces the displayed two-term bias identity and removes the recurring estimated-\(m_2\) cross-period remainder without requiring a transition-density estimator. A sequential transport correction is still needed because \(Q_2\) is interventional; it should be named rather than hidden inside \(\widehat T\).

The proposed rational transition pairing is normalized and makes the treated joint density cancel algebraically while retaining an \(\eta\epsilon\) target signal. In contrast, the present baseline-density perturbation contributes an additional smoothness/amplitude channel and cannot prove the advertised \(\alpha_1+t\) exponent.

The public Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele description already covers two-time-point g-formula HOIF estimation and reports minimax optimality in some anisotropic or structured settings, so generic “first longitudinal HOIF frontier” language is unsafe. The defensible distinction is the exact transition-\(L^1\)-Hölder model, estimator architecture, two-channel rate, and chained converse specified above. [EcoSta Abstract A0576](https://www.cmstatistics.org/RegistrationsV2/EcoSta2025/viewSubmission.php?in=576&token=8561n129qq6qo72qo5o1nq664nn5pqpo)

Cinelli et al. provide the correct named external consumer: optimality outside the root-\(n\) regime for time-varying treatments. [Cinelli et al., “A Dozen Challenges in Causality and Causal Inference”](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.17099)

note: Fresh gpt-5.6-sol medium audit after angle1-v1: exact equivalent bias forms, transformed transport product, corrected weighted stage-2/two-index transport chains, conjecture labeling, and honest state-sufficiency scope.
directive:
Retain the field-level novelty floor, C2, and C3, but revise C1 and all dependent claims as follows.

1. Correct fold conditioning and bias identity. Let F_T, F_TB, and F_TBG denote the sigma-fields generated by the nuisance, nuisance-plus-backward, and nuisance-plus-backward-plus-Gram folds. The fits (hat pi1,hat pi2,hat m2) are F_T-measurable, hat m1 is F_TB-measurable, and projection kernels are F_TBG-measurable. Condition the SDR bias on F_TB. Put w_j=pi_j/hat pi_j, e2=hat m2-m2, and T_g f(h1)=int f(h2)g(h2|h1)dh2. Then
b1^dagger=m1+T_g{(1-w2)e2},
and the exact equivalent identities are
E[hat psi_SDR-psi|F_TB]=E_Q1[(1-w1)(hat m1-b1^dagger)]+E_Q2[(1-w2)e2]
and
E[hat psi_SDR-psi|F_TB]=E_Q1[(1-w1)(hat m1-m1)]+E_Q1[w1 T_g{(1-w2)e2}].
The reviewer’s weighted expression and the unweighted Q2 expression are equivalent representations. Do not say the mixed error is eliminated. State the stage-local-plus-transport decomposition
B1=E_Q1[(1-w1)(hat m1-m1)], B2=E_Q2[(1-w2)e2], BT=-E_Q1[(1-w1)T_g{(1-w2)e2}], bias=B1+B2+BT.
Direct regression absorbs/transforms BT into hat m1-b1^dagger; an observable higher-order chain is still required.

2. Correct the observable chains. Retain omega=A1/hat pi1, R1=A1/hat pi1-1, S1=1-A1/hat pi1, T1=omega(hat D2-hat m1), S2=omega(1-A2/hat pi2), T2=omega A2(Y-hat m2)/hat pi2. Use the stage-two internal residual tilde R2=omega{A2/hat pi2-1}; never place R1 between K2 kernels. With k internal residuals define
Delta_1,k=(-1)^k U_E^{k+2} Sym[S1 K1 (R1 K1)^k T1],
Delta_2,k=(-1)^k U_E^{k+2} Sym[S2 K2 (tilde R2 K2)^k T2].
Use normalization (|E|)_q^{-1}. Replace the scalar transport family by
Delta_T,p,r=(-1)^{p+r} U_E^{p+r+3} Sym[S1 K1 (R1 K1)^p S2 K2 (tilde R2 K2)^r T2],
where the bridge observation carrying S2 joins backward through H1 and forward through H2. The shortest p=r=0 coefficient is +1; the current (-1)^3 sign is wrong. Condition analysis on F_TBG. Call the raw objects symmetrized cross-fitted U-statistics unless conditional Hoeffding centering is explicitly supplied. Use a finite multi-index set I_T and treat sufficiency of scalar q_T as open.

3. Relabel the attaining upper bound and matched lower bound as one headline Conjecture (NOT YET PROVED). Keep s1=alpha1+t, s2=alpha2+beta and rate n^-1/2+n^{-2s1/(2s1+d)}+n^{-2s2/(2s2+d)} as plausible, not established. Incoming C^t(L1) maps every bounded terminal integrand into C^t without estimating g, while the second channel is the ordinary Q2 bilinear channel. Explicitly list bounded Q1/Q2 design densities, overlap, clipping, independent Gram folds, projection stability, and a proved two-index transport-tail bound as proof inputs.

4. Add Robins-Li-Tchetgen Tchetgen-van der Vaart 2016TR (arXiv:1601.05820; published SPA version) separately. It already identifies HOIF applicability to time-dependent treatment with time-varying confounding, so make no first-longitudinal-HOIF claim. The proposed unproved addition is the incoming-state C^t(L1) transition model without outgoing-state smoothness, direct augmented-pseudo-outcome regression without fitting g, the two proposed channels, and normalized transition-mediated lower bounds. Do not claim disjointness, containment, failure, or dominance. Keep Bonvini-Kennedy-Keele separate and make no set-inclusion claim from its public abstract.

5. Preserve C2 and C3, but mark all affinity calculations, matched converse, and C3 root-n consequence as proof obligations/consequences of the conjectured frontier. Retain exact state sufficiency only as an explicit mathematical restriction. Do not invent a protocol or validation evidence. State that no empirical validation is supplied; applicability requires a future protocol to prespecify H2, document retained history, and assess the conditional-mean restriction, or define H2 to include the relevant recorded history while acknowledging the resulting dimension.

note: Angle1-v2 REJECT rebuild requirements: parametric root-n converse, positive affinity, explicit block projection, no invented protocol evidence, and normalized-pair smoothness audit.
directive: In the draft-rebuild, combine the prior exact-bias/two-index-transport directive with these mandatory repairs: (i) add a one-dimensional regular parametric submodel and prove the combined converse R_n >= c max{n^{-1/2},n^{-r1},n^{-r2}} before stating the root-n iff; (ii) state mixture affinity is uniformly bounded away from zero, or give an equivalent uniform TV/Hellinger upper bound, not merely “bounded affinity”; (iii) when nominal projection dimension exceeds an evaluation/Gram fold size, define an explicit block-diagonal or independently aggregated projection scheme with block sizes below fold size, separate Gram estimates, normalization, and the variance/remainder target—do not write undefined “block truncation”; (iv) remove all invented glucose-clamp protocol and validation claims and present exact state sufficiency only as an unvalidated structural model restriction; (v) audit the rational lower-bound denominators under asymmetric alpha/t and alpha2/beta, explicitly proving the resulting transition remains C^t(L1) and the outcome remains C^beta, or redesign the paired perturbation. Preserve C2 and C3 and the field novelty floor.
